intent: kill every running Android emulator on the machine (both docker-based and plain) to free resources
finding: User asked Claude to hunt down and kill Android emulators in two separate sessions within a few hours on 2026-04-17. In bf979f20 the user explicitly asked 'look up and kill local Android emulators that are running. look up docker-based ones, look up non-docker-based ones' and then 'Give me a command for killing all of them' — signalling they want a durable command rather than an ad hoc procedure. In b6c1e58f the very first message was 'is there an android emulator running? if so, kill it'. The outcome is concrete (free the machine of stale emulators), recurs across sessions/contexts, and is exactly the kind of thing a single script (shell one-liner wrapping adb emu kill / adb devices / docker ps filters / pkill qemu-system) could replace for all future runs. Tension: recurrence is 2 in the window, not >=3, so medium rather than high.
existing_mitigation: None found. No skill, hook, or CLAUDE.md rule covers emulator cleanup; no script in ~/.claude/ for it.

intent: test-drive the full Everything-Claude-Code plugin/skill suite inside one project without any of it bleeding into ~/.claude at the user level
finding: User spent much of session 4aae3e8e trying to install the full ECC suite at project scope only. Frustration was explicit and repeated: 'NO LOCAL ON PROJECT!!!!', 'IS IT INSTALLED IN MY USER????', 'I zapped it this morning, and was really annoyed'. Claude reached for user-level installs at least once and had to be stopped. In the follow-up 0c96a6ee the user opened a new project and immediately hit another leak: 'I don't want superpowers, how come they are active here locally?' The underlying task is the same outcome each time — spin up a sandboxed, project-scoped Claude Code environment with an arbitrary plugin set, verify nothing lands in ~/.claude, tear it down cleanly. That outcome is recurring (two projects in the same day) and fully replaceable by one durable artifact: a setup script / skill that sets CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR or project-level plugin config, installs the requested suite there, and verifies isolation. Severity tension: evidence includes explicit frustration (points toward critical/high) but recurrence is 2 sessions, and under-severity is preferred per policy — medium with frustration noted.
existing_mitigation: Partial: ~/.claude/skills/locked-down-headless-agent/ documents CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR isolation, but it is framed for headless/cron agents, not for interactive 'try this plugin bundle in one repo without touching my user' workflows. No skill specifically installs an ECC-style bundle at project scope.

intent: sweep a set of related repos at once — check everyone is on latest, merge any open PRs, push main — rather than doing each repo by hand
finding: The user repeatedly drove Claude through batch operations across multiple related git repos. In d93675df: 'are all the jhana repos on latest?', 'is the server on main', 'are there open PRs?', 'merge all and push changes to main'. In 90681993 the user asked Claude to enumerate all repos under ~/Repos with recent git activity and then add proposal-folder + MCP tooling to each one. The underlying outcome — 'bring a named group of repos to a known-good, all-merged, all-pushed state' — is clearly recurring (different repo group in each session) and automatable by a single script or skill that takes a list of repo paths and runs check-out/pull/merge-PRs/push per repo with a summary report at the end. Severity: medium because the outcome is valuable and the pattern held across two sessions with different repo groups, but the user did not explicitly ask for automation.
existing_mitigation: Partial: ~/.claude/skills/repo-autosync/ provides scheduled single-repo auto-commit+push via cron, but does not cover the 'sweep N related repos to green on demand' workflow — it is per-repo, unattended, and commit-focused rather than merge-focused.

intent: list the repos under ~/Repos that have recent git activity so I can act on them
finding: In 90681993 the user asked Claude twice, correcting itself, for essentially the same enumeration: 'look through my home folder No, sorry, look through the repo folder and List the ripos' then 'look at the ones that have recent commits or recent activity in Git and list them all for me please.' This is a precursor step for the multi-repo sweep (OBS-003) and comes up whenever the user wants to operate across their active projects. A tiny durable artifact (shell function or skill that lists repos under ~/Repos sorted by last-commit date) would replace the manual scan each time. Single session in this window, so low severity — worth logging as a building block for the broader multi-repo automation theme.
